_meta.credit: Structure adapted from borski/travel-hacking-toolkit (MIT) — github.com/borski/travel-hacking-toolkit. Data independently re-verified for 2026.
_meta.definition: An award hold reserves a saver seat for a window of hours/days BEFORE points are in your account, letting you transfer credit-card points without losing the seat. Only a handful of programs offer real holds.
_meta.strategy: Hold matters because (a) point transfers take minutes to 4 days and (b) saver inventory is volatile. AA's 24h hold + 1-2h Amex transfer is the standard playbook. For Marriott (3-7 day transfers) use LH M&M's 5-day hold.

allowed.booking_method: online (aa.com) for most; phone for partner awards on some routes
allowed.notes: Hold window reduced from 5 days to 24h on Apr 30, 2025. Hold NOT available within 6 days of departure or on Web Specials. Direct online self-serve is best in the industry.
5-day hold — longest of any major program. Critical for Marriott Bonvoy transfers (3-7 days). Select partner holds permitted.

allowed.cost: 0 for Cathay metal; USD 39 for partner awards
allowed.notes: 3-day hold; partner award holds cost extra.
3-day hold; useful since Avios programs all draw from same balance.
2-day hold but Turkish call centers are famously slow — budget time.
1-2 day hold, agent-dependent. Useful for ANA F bookings (which are phone-only anyway).
Not guaranteed — agent's call. Worth asking on Suites awards.

not_allowed.notes: No holds; points must be in account at booking.
No holds. 24-hour cancellation policy is the closest analog (refundable within 24h).
No formal hold; 24-hour cancellation grace period instead.
No holds; points must be in account before booking.
No holds; family-pool transfers + 5-day ticketing window are the workaround.
No holds; LifeMiles+ subscribers get instant 10% discount but no hold mechanism.
Online bookings cannot be held — phone only (see allowed list).
Routes transitioning to Alaska Atmos through Apr 2026; legacy hold not available.
